channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: ‘We must do more to protect our credentials’: Password security has barely changed since 2015 — and that's a big problem for everyone
item.description: Password habits are improving, but not fast enough to keep accounts secure, experts warn.

item.title: The Most Dangerous Molecule in ‘Star Trek’ History
item.description: As 'Starfleet Academy' sets itself on some high stakes for its season one finale, it's returning to one of the most fearsome ideas from 'Trek' past.

item.title: M5 Max MacBook Pro Review: Preeminent Power In the Same Old Shell
item.description: Apple’s enormously expensive M5 Max MacBook Pro has more power than the laptop can handle, literally.

item.title: ‘He is a once-in-a-thousand-years kind of person’: Tim Cook explains how the Steve Jobs reality distortion field convinced him to join Apple
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook opens up about the company's history, DNA, and why he joined despite the company's then-sorry state.

item.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber will step aside
item.description: Jay Graber became the first CEO of Bluesky in 2021 when the network and its AT Protocol spun out from life as a Twitter research project to go independent, but now she's leaving that role, as reported previously by Wired. While venture capitalist and former Automattic CEO Toni Schneider steps in as interim CEO until […]

item.title: These Self-Configuring Modular Robots May One Day Rule the World
item.description: Billions of possible body plans, all made entirely of legs.

item.title: Bluesky's CEO is stepping down after nearly 5 years
item.description: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber, who has led the upstart social platform since 2021, is stepping down from her role as its top executive. Toni Schneider, who has been an advisor and investor in Bluesky, will take over the job temporarily while Graber stays on as Chief Innovation Officer. "As Bluesky matures, the company needs a seasoned operator focused on scaling and execution, while I return to what I do best: building new things," Graber wrote in a blog post. Schneider, who was previously CEO at Wordpress parent Automattic, will be that "experienced operator and leader" while Blueksy's board searche...

item.title: You can get three months of Disney Plus and Hulu for $15
item.description: If you’re looking for a way to stream the Oscars this weekend, or catch up on nominees like The Secret Agent, the Disney Plus and Hulu bundle is worth a look. The ceremony will stream live on Hulu for the first time on March 15th, and right now, new and eligible returning subscribers can get […]

item.title: 'For many years, Europe has relied on American software and therefore created a certain risk of dependency': European alternative to Microsoft Office launches — but will this open source champion be good enough to topple Word and Google Docs?
item.description: Office.eu offers a European cloud workspace built on Nextcloud and Collabora Online infrastructure hosted entirely on regional data centers.

item.title: Nourrir des astronautes sur la Lune et Mars ? Ces expériences rapprochent un vieux rêve de la réalité
item.description: Produire de la nourriture sur la Lune ou sur Mars sera indispensable pour de futures missions habitées de longue durée. Mais ces mondes sont recouverts d’un sol poussiéreux et hostile à la vie. Des chercheurs explorent aujourd’hui différentes stratégies pour transformer ce régolithe en un...

item.title: Novo Nordisk and Hims End Their Ozempic War. Here’s What It Means for You
item.description: The companies' new partnership could signal the beginning of the end for compounded GLP-1s.

item.title: Here's a record-low price for the Google Pixel Watch 4 — 'a great smart companion' that we scored 4.5 stars
item.description: Score the Pixel Watch 4 for an unbeatable $339.99

item.title: Apple’s new M5 Max feels like a huge upgrade if you bought your laptop three years ago
item.description: We've been busy testing many new MacBooks, ranging from the new $1,099 M5 MacBook Air, going all the way up the $6,149 16-inch MacBook Pro with the M5 Max chip. While these computers are identical in design to last year's models, they have some things in common: neither delivers a significant lead over their M4 […]

item.title: Apple Studio Display XDR review: pro at a premium
item.description: It's been almost exactly four years since Apple released the 5K Studio Display that so many wanted, even if it didn't really deliver as a high-end display. Apple has now revamped the Studio Display lineup, sort of, with another Studio Display and the Studio Display XDR. They're both 27-inch 5K (5,120 x 2,880) displays with […]

item.title: 'There can be no justification': Chinese automaker apologizes to Land Rover after another big copycat controversy
item.description: Chinese automaker admits full responsibility for ad campaign that looks eerily similar to Range Rover promotional material.

item.title: Apple’s latest MacBook Pro surges ahead for battery life — but with one disappointing surprise
item.description: The 16-inch MacBook Pro with M5 Pro offers excellent battery life, according to our tests.

item.title: The best-selling Hatch Restore 3 can help you survive daylight saving — and it's currently on sale at Amazon
item.description: Survive daylight saving time with the best-selling Hatch Restore 3 alarm clock, which can help you go to bed and wake up more easily.

item.title: Pénurie de composants : pourquoi le conflit en Iran menace bien plus que la RAM
item.description: On pensait que l'IA était notre plus gros problème d'approvisionnement en mémoire. On se trompait. Entre la demande délirante des serveurs de Nvidia et maintenant le conflit en Iran qui menace les matières premières, votre futur PC va coûter encore plus cher.

item.title: Apple reportedly delays its planned smart display launch to fall
item.description: Mark Gurman at Bloomberg is back with the latest rumors about what's afoot with Apple's future plans, and how its ongoing difficulties with artificial intelligence seem to be creating further delays for its next wave of product launches. His sources say that Apple is expected to postpone the debut of its smart home display until later in 2026, likely September when it often introduces new gadgets. Although the hardware has reportedly been finished for months, this delay is being credited to the company's AI-centric overhaul of Siri still not being complete.The device, internally known as J490,...

item.title: Arnold Schwarzenegger Will Be Back… as Conan the Barbarian
item.description: His long-discussed 'King Conan' sequel is moving forward with 'Mission: Impossible' director Christopher McQuarrie.

item.title: One of this rugged phone’s cameras is a pop-out action cam
item.description: What if your smartphone's camera wasn't locked to the back of the device? Honor's Robot Phone, which we got to see in action at MWC 2026, features a camera attached to a stabilized gimbal arm that can pop-up and look around. But it's still permanently tethered to the phone. The RugOne Xsnap 7 Pro, a […]

item.title: Anthropic launches code review tool to check flood of AI-generated code
item.description: Anthropic launched Code Review in Claude Code, a multi-agent system that automatically analyzes AI-generated code, flags logic errors, and helps enterprise developers manage the growing volume of code produced with AI.

item.title: Apple plans a high-end Ultra line, including iPhone Fold, report says
item.description: A new line of high-end products could be on the way from Apple.

item.title: ‘Lightning-in-a-Box’ Concept Could Shrink a Thunderstorm to the Size of Your Thumb
item.description: If the concept comes to life, scientists would have a field day exploring lightning with remarkable ease.

item.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber steps down
item.description: Bluesky's board will search for a replacement while Graber becomes Chief Innovation Officer.

item.title: ‘Cash Apples’ is giving away $500,000 to people who click on trees in a web browser
item.description: Want to make real money just by clicking on virtual trees? Starting today at 1PM PT / 4PM ET, residents of the United States can begin an online hunt for golden apples in a cutesy web game - collect enough of them, and Cash App will send you $40. You can repeat that five times […]

item.title: Mr. President, please take off your hat
item.description: Trump is wearing branded merch during the country’s most solemn moments. The remains of six U.S. service members killed in Kuwait following the U.S. strike on Iran were returned home Sunday during a dignified transfer at the Dover Air Force Base in Delaware. President Donald Trump wore a branded hat for the occasion.

item.title: Pourquoi le Pixel 11 Pro Fold ne nous fait pas encore rêver
item.description: On prend les mêmes et on recommence ? Les premiers rendus du Pixel 11 Pro Fold viennent de fuiter, et le moins que l'on puisse dire, c'est que Google ne semble pas pressé de révolutionner sa copie.

item.title: Anthropic sues Pentagon as Claude downloads soar
item.description: Anthropic sues the Defense Department as app usage soars

item.title: ‘American Power will finally stand up in cyberspace’: Trump unveils new cyber strategy he says will “deploy the full suite of US government defensive and offensive cyber operations”
item.description: The Trump administration has revealed its US cyber strategy for the first time, and it is very 'America First'.

item.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber Is Stepping Down
item.description: Venture capitalist Toni Schneider will take over as interim CEO as Bluesky’s board of directors hunts for a permanent replacement.

item.title: Australians are turning to VPN Super Unlimited Proxy to for age verification — but there are much more trustworthy alternatives
item.description: If you're worried about your personal data, make sure you're using a VPN you can trust

item.title: ‘This was about principle, not people’—OpenAI’s robotics hardware lead resigns
item.description: Caitlin Kalinowski cited concerns about the company’s new agreement with the Pentagon. Caitlin Kalinowski, an OpenAI employee who oversaw hardware within the robotics division, is leaving the company. Kalinowski’s decision came shortly after OpenAI’s deal with the Pentagon was announced in late February.In a post on social media, Kalinowski explained that the decision was about “principle” in regard to the recent deal. “I care deeply about the Robotics team and the work we built together. This wasn’t an easy call,” Kalinowski wrote. “AI has an important role in national security. But surveilla...

item.title: It looks like the DOJ isn’t going to break up Live Nation and Ticketmaster
item.description: The DOJ and Live Nation agreed to a tentative settlement, but dozens of State Attorneys General don't want to drop the lawsuit.

item.title: Uber expands its program that helps pair women riders and drivers
item.description:
Uber has expanded its program that helps pair women riders and drivers. The Women Preferences feature is now available nationwide, after being tested in several cities. It has previously been available in many countries around the globe and started in Saudi Arabia back in 2019. It's pretty easy to use. Women riders will see an option for Women Drivers when requesting a trip, and this also works when making a reservation in advance. Users can also make a preference for a woman driver in the settings app, though this doesn't guarantee anything and depends on the driver pool.
The feature works i...

item.title: Is your brand of horny normal? Feelds new quiz will help you figure it out.
item.description: The "Reflections" tool from Feeld lets you share your personal desires and boundaries via a custom link.
